title: Utilities Transformed: The Role of IT in Reshaping the Energy Business, 1998 Edition
author: Aberdeen Group (Barton Taylor)
source_file: 1998 Utilities Transformed- The Role of IT in Reshaping the Energy Business, 1998 Edition.pdf
abstract: This 102-page Aberdeen Group report by analyst Barton Taylor examines the role of information technology in the deregulating U.S. utility industry, covering electricity and natural gas markets. The study analyzes IT applications across generation, transmission, distribution, and power marketing segments, with particular focus on OASIS electricity transmission reservation systems, SCADA/energy management systems, AMR (Automatic Meter Reading) networks, and the internet as a platform for competitive energy markets. The report profiles 19 vendor organizations and predicts that ultimate winners in energy will be companies that leverage IT as a competitive weapon rather than merely low-cost producers.
importance_rationale: Published at the inception of U.S. electricity deregulation, this report was one of the first comprehensive analyses of IT's role in reshaping competitive energy markets; Aberdeen's identification of OASIS, AMR, and internet-based energy services as pivotal proved prescient and the vendor profiling captured a unique market snapshot during the deregulation wave.
relevance_rationale: U.S. electricity and natural gas deregulation, AMR networks (evolved into smart meters/AMI), SCADA/energy management systems, and internet-based energy services are all directly relevant to current grid modernization, energy transition, and smart grid initiatives; the framework of IT-enabled competition in energy remains a live issue in 2026.
prescience_rationale: The report's central prediction — that IT-leveraging companies rather than low-cost producers would win competitive energy markets — was validated by the rise of competitive retail energy providers, smart metering companies, and energy information platforms; the OASIS transmission reservation system prediction was accurate; AMR evolved into advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) exactly as predicted.
